title: The Destroyer in the Geofront
wikitext:
[]: The Destroyer in the Geofront             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Mar 04, 2007 12:13 am Okay, so everyone remembers Asuka picking it up and throwing it at the JSSDF. I assume its presence during the series hasn't gone unnoticed, either. What I want to know is, what is it? The only marking is the word 'NERV' on the bows and transom, which isn't terribly helpful. The vessel in question looks like a generic WWII model; the funnel arrangement reminds me of the Fletcher class and its Sumner and Gearing variants, but the hull profile doesn't match any of them. It doesn't look like any Japanese design I know, nor does it look much like the typical postwar missile boats ubiquitous today. It appears to have some hardware missing aft of the superstructure, rather like the Gearing destroyers with FRAM Mark I modifications, but I'm pretty sure it isn't one of those. Anyone else know what design it is, or might be? And just how did they get it into the Geofront to begin with? Why? (On a related note, what's up with the gunboat anchored in Lilith's LCL pool?)

[]: Re: The Destroyer in the Geofront             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 04, 2007 12:23 am BobBQ wrote: Okay, so everyone remembers Kyoko-sama picking it up and throwing it at the JSSDF.

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Sun Mar 04, 2007 12:35 am not counting your crazy answers im gonna say its there because someone was compensating for something

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sun Mar 04, 2007 1:22 am It's there because Gendo likes boat rides!

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Mar 04, 2007 10:20 am Defectron wrote: It's there because Gendo likes boat rides!

[]: Re: The Destroyer in the Geofront             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Mar 04, 2007 11:29 am I was hoping I could use this in TToR404, but it looks like it'll take some serious fanwanking. Unfortunately, Gainax / ProdIG were pretty sloppy with the artwork and the ship's proportions and layout change drastically from shot to shot. It also underwent a makeover between D&R and EoE. (Apologies for the poor visuals; my image editor is malfunctioning.) Episode 19: Rebirth Original (Unknown Fansub): End of Evangelion: I'm still pretty sure it's either a late WWII (with retrofitted masts and sonar dome) or early Cold War design; two pipes and two forward turrets aren't commonly seen on newer hulls. Reichu wrote: It's a frigate.

[]:             Quote Post by x1berserk » Sun Mar 04, 2007 1:29 pm How does one move a welded hull in pieces? Was it cut into three sections, resulting in the partial splitting when Team Soryu picked it up?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Mar 04, 2007 2:33 pm I imagine that the best way to get the destroyers down to the geofront and Terminal Dogma would simply have been to assemble them onsite. As to why they were down there, I can only assume it would be a good idea to have a gunboat of some description on large bodies of water. Just in case.

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Sun Mar 04, 2007 8:01 pm but if 1 gunboat is able to defend an enemy that defeated 3 eva's, you got some serious problems.

[]:             Quote Post by BLACKANGEL32076 » Mon Mar 05, 2007 2:29 am A gun boat? With two turrets the superstructure and bow? Not likely. The ship that Unit-02 picks up has a light deck and would be too big to be a gunboat to have one of those AND two turrets. If anything, she was a heavily modified Allen M. Sumner (DD-692)-class Destroyer picked up for cheap from some U.S. ally that we sold her to.

[]: Sigh....             Quote Post by reishin8amara » Tue Aug 24, 2010 8:55 pm Alright,seeing as I am getting sick of speculation on this Battleship topic....I shall point out the obvious note you all seem to be missing....THAT BOAT is the same boat that Gendo Ikari and his friend remeet on during the trip to Antartica a while after 2nd Impact occured. I forget the other mans name but he was the one that asked Gendo about Yui. It makes sense that boat would be down there.It went to antartica,thus the boat probally had much research material in and "on" it including samples and it was just impossible to move it all.

[]: Re: Sigh....             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Tue Aug 24, 2010 9:57 pm reishin8amara wrote: Gendo Ikari and his friend
